selection_rationale: High-value reusable QA and implementation incidents spanning environment issues, logic errors, contract drift, null-safe QA, join-cardinality failures, and incremental performance regressions.

problem.symptom: QA runbook blocked by deferred schema resolution, Redshift view materialization instability, and missing MetricFlow CLI

resolution.fix: Use no-defer strict compile/build, apply bind=false to the affected view, and validate MetricFlow through a compatible Python tool environment
resolution.root_cause: Mixed execution-context and runtime-tooling issues rather than business-logic failure

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: New columns exist in dev but deferred refs lag, view materialization fails intermittently, or MetricFlow tooling is unavailable in the repo runtime
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: Stable single-environment runs with native MetricFlow support

problem.symptom: Downstream model referenced non-existent upstream summary columns

resolution.fix: Derive the needed fields downstream from existing canonical inputs instead of adding placeholder upstream columns
resolution.root_cause: Downstream model implied ownership of non-canonical derived fields that did not belong in the upstream summary

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: PR review flags missing upstream columns that can be derived locally
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: The upstream field is truly canonical and shared broadly

problem.symptom: Contract-enforced mart failed due to schema-type drift and a quality threshold regression

resolution.fix: Align contract types with compiled output and widen the decrypt window used for QA revalidation
resolution.root_cause: Schema-contract drift combined with an overly narrow runtime window

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: Contract marts fail after upstream type changes or runtime window changes affect quality gates
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: Business intent requires preserving the declared contract regardless of compiled output

problem.symptom: QA mismatch rate was driven by NULL comparison semantics rather than data drift

resolution.fix: Use NULL-safe equality with coalesce in sparse cohort comparison queries
resolution.root_cause: Legacy QA query compared NULL-heavy measures with direct equality

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: Sparse metric comparisons where both sides may be NULL
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: NULL and zero have materially different business meanings

problem.symptom: Massive duplicate-row failures in downstream tests due to enrichment join fanout

resolution.fix: Deduplicate enrichment sources with row_number() before joining
resolution.root_cause: ODS enrichment sources returned multiple rows where a 1:1 assumption was being made

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: Enrichment joins assume 1:1 cardinality against operational sources
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: History preservation is required and duplicates are expected

problem.symptom: Valid failures were dropped because a nullable field was used as a restrictive filter

resolution.fix: Remove the restrictive null filter and coalesce the nullable field explicitly
resolution.root_cause: Implicitly treating NULL as invalid when NULL still represented valid business cases

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: Counting statuses or failures where the descriptive field may be NULL for valid records
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: NULL truly indicates corrupt or unusable data

problem.symptom: Incremental runtime degraded severely after backfill due to missing predicate pushdown

resolution.fix: Push date filters into referenced CTEs used by incremental joins
resolution.root_cause: Incremental runs still forced full scans of large referenced CTEs

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: Incremental models slow down after backfills or late-arrival logic expansions
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: Relevant upstream CTEs are already tightly predicate-limited
